name: Malaria
description: A parasitic disease caused by Plasmodium parasites, spread through bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. One of the leading causes of illness and death in Uganda.
spreads: Through bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. Not spread person to person.
prevention: Sleep under an insecticide-treated mosquito net every night
Use mosquito repellent on exposed skin especially at dusk and dawn
Eliminate standing water around your home where mosquitoes breed
urgency_reason: Malaria can become fatal within 24 hours if untreated. Go to a health facility immediately.

name: Typhoid Fever
differentiating_symptoms: Slow heart rate relative to fever
description: A life-threatening bacterial infection caused by Salmonella Typhi, common in areas with poor sanitation.
spreads: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food or water.
prevention: Drink boiled or treated water
Wash hands with soap after using the toilet
Eat hot, well-cooked food
urgency_reason: Can cause intestinal perforation or severe internal bleeding if untreated.

name: Cholera
description: An acute diarrheal infection caused by ingestion of food or water contaminated with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae.
spreads: Contaminated water sources and poor fecal-waste management.
prevention: Drink safe, treated water
Practice frequent handwashing with soap
Cook food thoroughly and peel fruits
urgency_reason: Can cause severe dehydration and death within hours if not treated immediately.

name: Tuberculosis (TB)
description: A serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs, caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria.
spreads: Through the air when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or speaks.
prevention: BCG vaccination at birth
Ensure good ventilation in living spaces
Cover mouth and nose when coughing or sneezing
urgency_reason: Highly contagious and can lead to permanent lung damage or death without a full course of antibiotics.

name: HIV/AIDS
description: A virus that attacks the body’s immune system (CD4 cells). If untreated, it progresses to AIDS.
spreads: Unprotected sexual contact, needle sharing, and from mother to child during birth or breastfeeding.
prevention: Consistent and correct use of condoms
Use of sterile needles and medical equipment
Taking PrEP or PEP medications if at risk
urgency_reason: Requires lifelong antiretroviral therapy (ART) to prevent fatal opportunistic infections.

name: Bilharzia (Schistosomiasis)
differentiating_symptoms: Itchy skin after water contact (swimmer's itch)
description: A disease caused by parasitic worms that live in fresh water snails in tropical regions.
spreads: Skin contact with contaminated fresh water (lakes, rivers) containing infected snails.
prevention: Avoid swimming or wading in infested fresh water
Boil or filter water used for bathing
Use proper latrines to prevent water contamination
urgency_reason: Chronic infection can lead to liver failure, bladder cancer, or kidney damage.

name: Sleeping Sickness
description: African Trypanosomiasis, a parasitic disease transmitted by the tsetse fly in sub-Saharan Africa.
spreads: Bites of infected tsetse flies.
prevention: Wear long-sleeved clothing in neutral colors
Use insect repellent in fly-infested areas
Avoid bushes where tsetse flies rest during the day
urgency_reason: Once the parasite enters the central nervous system, it is fatal without specialized treatment.

name: Measles
differentiating_symptoms: Rash starting at the hairline and spreading down
description: A highly contagious viral infection that is a major cause of death among young children globally.
spreads: Airborne droplets from coughing, sneezing, or breathing.
prevention: Ensure children receive two doses of the MMR vaccine
Isolate infected persons during the contagious period
Vitamin A supplementation for infected children
urgency_reason: Can cause severe complications like pneumonia, blindness, or brain swelling (encephalitis).

name: Meningitis
differentiating_symptoms: Inability to touch chin to chest
description: Inflammation of the protective membranes (meninges) covering the brain and spinal cord.
spreads: Exchange of respiratory and throat secretions (kissing, coughing, or sharing utensils).
prevention: Vaccination against meningococcus, pneumococcus, and Hib
Maintain good hand hygiene
Avoid sharing personal items like drinks or cigarettes
urgency_reason: Bacterial meningitis can be fatal within 24 hours or cause permanent brain damage.

name: Rabies
differentiating_symptoms: Tingling at the site of an old bite
description: A viral disease most often transmitted through the bite of a rabid animal, usually a dog.
spreads: Saliva of an infected animal entering a wound or mucous membranes.
prevention: Vaccinate pets (dogs and cats) annually
Wash any animal bite wound immediately with soap and water
Seek Post-Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) immediately after a bite
urgency_reason: Once symptoms appear, rabies is 100% fatal. Immediate treatment after exposure is vital.

name: Brucellosis
differentiating_symptoms: Undulant fever (rises and falls over weeks)
description: A bacterial infection that spreads from animals to people, often through raw dairy products.
spreads: Consuming unpasteurized dairy, undercooked meat, or contact with fluids of infected livestock.
prevention: Avoid unpasteurized milk and cheese
Cook meat thoroughly
Wear gloves and masks when handling farm animals
urgency_reason: Can cause long-term complications in the heart, brain, and joints if not treated with specific antibiotics.

name: Hepatitis B
differentiating_symptoms: Yellowing of the whites of the eyes
description: A serious liver infection caused by the Hepatitis B virus (HBV).
spreads: Contact with infected blood, semen, or other body fluids; mother to child at birth.
prevention: Get the full series of the Hepatitis B vaccine
Practice safe sex with condoms
Avoid sharing needles, razors, or toothbrushes
urgency_reason: Chronic infection can lead to liver cirrhosis, liver failure, or liver cancer.

name: Pneumonia
differentiating_symptoms: Chest pain that worsens with deep breaths
Productive cough with thick yellow/green mucus
description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
spreads: Airborne droplets from coughing or sneezing.
prevention: Get vaccinated (Pneumococcal and Flu vaccines)
Practice frequent handwashing
Avoid smoking, which weakens lung defenses
urgency_reason: Can cause severe respiratory failure and sepsis, especially in children and the elderly.

name: Dengue Fever
description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing severe flu-like illness, sometimes leading to hemorrhagic fever.
spreads: Through the bite of an infected Aedes mosquito.
prevention: Remove standing water around homes
Wear long-sleeved clothing
Use mosquito repellents and window screens
urgency_reason: Severe Dengue (hemorrhagic) can lead to shock, internal bleeding, and death if not managed.

name: Dysentery
differentiating_symptoms: Visible blood and mucus in stool
description: An intestinal inflammation, especially in the colon, that can lead to severe diarrhea with mucus or blood.
spreads: Fecal-oral route through contaminated food, water, or poor hand hygiene.
prevention: Wash hands with soap frequently
Drink only safe or treated water
Ensure proper disposal of human waste
urgency_reason: Rapid blood and fluid loss can lead to shock and death, especially in small children.

name: Rift Valley Fever
description: A viral zoonotic disease that primarily affects animals but can also infect humans.
spreads: Contact with blood or organs of infected animals; mosquito bites.
prevention: Wear protective gear when handling livestock or animal tissue
Avoid consuming raw milk or meat
Use mosquito repellents and nets
urgency_reason: A small percentage develop ocular disease, encephalitis, or fatal hemorrhagic fever.

name: Severe Malnutrition
description: A deficiency of protein, energy, and micronutrients, commonly presenting as Kwashiorkor or Marasmus.
spreads: Not contagious; caused by inadequate food intake or poor absorption.
prevention: Promote exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months
Provide a varied diet rich in proteins and vitamins
Regular deworming of children
urgency_reason: Severely malnourished children are at very high risk of death from minor infections or heart failure.

name: Sickle Cell Disease
differentiating_symptoms: Painful swelling of hands and feet (dactylitis)
description: An inherited red blood cell disorder where cells become C-shaped and block blood flow.
spreads: Genetic; inherited from both parents carrying the trait.
prevention: Seek genetic counseling before having children
Early screening of newborns
Daily folic acid and prophylactic antibiotics as prescribed
urgency_reason: A 'sickle cell crisis' can lead to organ damage, stroke, or severe infection requiring hospitalization.

name: Hypertension
differentiating_symptoms: Pounding sensation in the chest or head
description: A chronic condition where the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
spreads: Not contagious; caused by lifestyle factors, diet, and genetics.
prevention: Reduce salt intake in diet
Exercise regularly for 30 minutes a day
Maintain a healthy body weight
urgency_reason: Often called the 'silent killer' because it can lead to sudden heart attack or stroke without warning.

name: Snake Bite
description: An injury caused by a bite from a snake, which may or may not inject venom.
spreads: Physical bite from a snake.
prevention: Wear boots and long pants in tall grass
Use a flashlight when walking outdoors at night
Clear bushes and tall grass from around the house
urgency_reason: Venom can cause respiratory arrest, bleeding disorders, or tissue death within minutes.

name: COVID-19
differentiating_symptoms: Sudden loss of sense of smell/taste
description: An infectious respiratory disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.
spreads: Respiratory droplets and aerosols through coughing, sneezing, or talking.
prevention: Get vaccinated and keep up with boosters
Wear a mask in crowded or poorly ventilated areas
Wash hands frequently with soap and water
urgency_reason: Can cause severe pneumonia and systemic organ failure, requiring hospitalization.

name: Ringworm
description: A common fungal skin infection, not actually caused by a worm.
spreads: Skin-to-skin contact, contact with animals, or sharing items like towels and combs.
prevention: Keep skin clean and dry
Don't share personal items like hairbrushes or towels
Wear shoes in public locker rooms and showers
urgency_reason: Not life-threatening but highly contagious and requires treatment to stop it from spreading.

name: Scabies
differentiating_symptoms: Itching that becomes unbearable at night
description: An itchy skin condition caused by a tiny burrowing mite called Sarcoptes scabiei.
spreads: Prolonged skin-to-skin contact; sharing bedding or clothing.
prevention: Wash clothes and bedding in hot water
Avoid close contact with infected persons
Treat all household members at the same time
urgency_reason: Extremely contagious and uncomfortable, leading to secondary skin infections if untreated.

name: Conjunctivitis
differentiating_symptoms: Eyelids glued shut in the morning
Pink/Red color in the whites of eyes
description: Inflammation or infection of the outer membrane of the eyeball and the inner eyelid (Pink Eye).
spreads: Direct contact with infected eye secretions or contaminated hands/surfaces.
prevention: Wash hands frequently with soap
Avoid touching or rubbing the eyes
Don't share towels, pillows, or eye makeup
urgency_reason: Highly contagious and can cause corneal damage if the cause is bacterial and untreated.

name: Whooping Cough
differentiating_symptoms: Coughing fits that lead to turning blue
description: Pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory tract infection characterized by a severe hacking cough.
spreads: Respiratory droplets when an infected person coughs or sneezes.
prevention: Get the DTaP/Tdap vaccination and boosters
Keep infants away from people with a cough
Prophylactic antibiotics for exposed family members
urgency_reason: Can be life-threatening for infants who may stop breathing during coughing fits.
